name: Science Fiction
description: Landmark science fiction novels, films, and TV series from H.G. Wells to modern streaming

questions.q: Which H.G. Wells novel, published in 1898, features a Martian invasion of Earth?

source.notes: Orson Welles's 1938 radio adaptation famously caused a panic.

questions.q: What is the name of the murderous AI in 2001: A Space Odyssey?

source.notes: HAL is each letter shifted one ahead from IBM.

questions.q: What is the name of Han Solo's iconic ship in Star Wars?

source.notes: A heavily modified YT-1300 light freighter.

questions.q: Who directed the 1982 film Blade Runner?

source.notes: Also directed Alien (1979) and The Martian (2015).

questions.q: What is the time-travel vehicle in Doctor Who called?

source.notes: Stands for Time And Relative Dimension In Space.

questions.q: Which actor plays Neo in The Matrix?

source.notes: Will Smith famously turned down the role.

questions.q: What is the name of the desert planet at the center of Frank Herbert's Dune?

source.notes: Source of the all-important spice melange.

questions.q: In which 1920 Czech play does the word 'robot' first appear?

source.notes: Written by Karel Čapek; from Czech 'robota' meaning forced labor.

questions.q: Who wrote the 1949 dystopian novel 1984?

source.notes: Pen name of Eric Blair; published a year before his death.

questions.q: In Douglas Adams's Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, what number is the Answer to the Ultimate Question of Life, the Universe, and Everything?

source.notes: The supercomputer Deep Thought reveals this answer.

questions.q: What are the bio-engineered androids called in Blade Runner?

source.notes: Manufactured by the Tyrell Corporation.

questions.q: Who wrote the Foundation series of science-fiction novels?

source.notes: Also known for the Three Laws of Robotics.

questions.q: In the Alien franchise, who plays Warrant Officer Ellen Ripley?

source.notes: Nominated for Best Actress for Aliens (1986).

questions.q: Which 1982 science-fiction classic stars Steven Spielberg's alien trying to 'phone home'?
choices: Close Encounters of the Third Kind

source.notes: Highest-grossing film of all time until Jurassic Park (1993).

questions.q: Which author wrote the 1984 cyberpunk novel Neuromancer?

source.notes: First novel to win the Hugo, Nebula, and Philip K. Dick Awards.

questions.q: Who plays Captain Jean-Luc Picard in Star Trek: The Next Generation?

source.notes: Reprised the role in the 2020s series Picard.

questions.q: Whose 1932 novel Brave New World depicts a highly-engineered future society?

source.notes: Title taken from Shakespeare's The Tempest.

questions.q: In what dark parallel dimension does much of Stranger Things' mystery unfold?

source.notes: A rotten, parallel version of Hawkins inhabited by monsters.

questions.q: Which Philip K. Dick novel was adapted into the 1982 film Blade Runner?
choices: The Man in the High Castle
Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

source.original_a: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?
source.notes: Originally published in 1968.

questions.q: What is the name of the spaceship in the Firefly television series?

source.notes: A Firefly-class transport ship; also the title of the 2005 feature film.

questions.q: Who wrote the 1969 feminist science-fiction classic The Left Hand of Darkness?

source.notes: Set on the planet Gethen, whose inhabitants are ambisexual.

questions.q: In The Matrix, what is Neo's legal/birth name?

source.notes: Works as a programmer by day; hacker by night.
